segments.text: Colleagues, today I rise to adjourn in memory of Mark Salzberg, a noted progressive activist, neighborhood leader, and beloved member of the Westside and Venice community, who passed away on June 27th after a long illness.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: Mark dedicated his life to civic engagement and local democracy.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: Mark was a leader in the democratic community and a founding member of the West Los Angeles Democrats in 2004,
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: where he served as president and became a trusted advisor to elected officials at every level, local, county, state, and federal.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: A pillar of the club, Mark also founded its Environment and Clean Energy Committee.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: Long before climate change was at the center of political debate, he was pushing Democrats to prioritize clean energy and environmental stewardship, bringing in expert speakers, advancing resolutions, and ensuring that the future of the planet was part of every conversation.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: His civic imagination extended to cultural projects.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: Mark supported efforts to create a Venice Museum and a historic trolley car, advocated for a Pacific electric heritage display near the Centennial Library, and even mediated proposals for new attractions at Venice Beach.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: A graduate of Carleton College, Mark built a career in technology as a project manager, bringing products to market.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: But it was his passion for civic life, for bringing neighbors together, strengthening democracy, and preserving this city's unique culture that defined his public service.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: Today, we are honored to be joined in council chambers by Mark's wife, Nancy, and by Kara Robin of the West LA Democratic Club, representing the community that Mark helped build and sustain.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: On behalf of the Los Angeles City Council, I extend our heartfelt condolences to Nancy.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: to Jenny and Alex, to the Salzburg family, and to all who mourn.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: In recognition of his many contributions to our city, I respectfully ask that the Los Angeles City Council adjourn today's meeting in memory of Mark Salzburg.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: May his memory be a blessing, and may his legacy of progressive advocacy and community leadership continue to inspire us all.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: Thank you.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_54

segments.text: Thank you so much, Council Member Yaroslavsky.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_41

segments.text: We are adjourned, and thank you so much to the Salzburg family for being with us.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_41
